originalTitle: Mali defence minister killed in rebel attacks, reports say
title: Mali defence minister killed in rebel attacks, reports say
content: A wave of coordinated attacks by jihadist militants and separatists has spread through the country.

originalTitle: Kenya's Sawe smashes two-hour barrier to make history at London Marathon
title: Our African Super Runner Makes History!
content:
Hello Super Runners!

Hi everyone! Do you like to run and play? Running is super fun, right? Well, guess what? We have some incredibly exciting news from our amazing continent, Africa! It's about a very special runner who made all of us proud. Get ready to cheer loudly for him! He did something truly amazing that no one had ever done before in a big race. It's a story of speed, strength, and making history.

Who is Sabastian Sawe?

Let's meet our hero! His name is Sabastian Sawe. Can you say that? Sa-bas-tian Sa-we! He is a super-fast runner, full of energy and determination. Sabastian comes from a beautiful country called Kenya. Kenya is right here in Africa, and it's famous for something very special. Can you guess what it is? Did you know? Kenya is known for having some of the fastest long-distance runners in the whole wide world! Many champions who run very, very far come from Kenya. Sabastian loves to run more than anything. He wakes up early and trains very hard almost every day. He eats healthy food and drinks lots of water to keep his body strong. This helps him run incredibly fast and be ready for big races.

What is a Marathon?

Now, let's talk about what Sabastian did. He ran in something called a marathon. What is a marathon? It's a very, very, VERY long running race! Imagine running around your school field. A marathon is like running around that field many, many, many times without stopping! It's about 42 kilometres long. That's a super far distance, like running from your town to another town far away! Running a marathon needs lots and lots of practice. It needs amazing energy and a big, brave heart that never gives up. Runners train for months and months to be ready for such a long journey. Did you know? The word “marathon” comes from an old story in a faraway land about a messenger who ran a very long way to share important news! It's a race with a long history.

Running Super Fast!

Sabastian ran a marathon. But he didn't just run it. Oh no, he ran it incredibly, unbelievably fast! He finished the entire super-long race in less than two hours! Can you imagine running for two whole hours without stopping, and running super fast the entire time? That's like running from the time you wake up until almost breakfast time, all in one go, without a break! Before Sabastian, no one had ever run a marathon that fast in a big race with many other runners trying to win. No one in the whole wide world had ever done it! He was the first! He didn't just win; he broke a record. A record means he did something better and faster than anyone else had ever done before. It's like being the first one to build the tallest sandcastle on the beach!

The Big Race in London

So, where did Sabastian do this amazing run? He did it in a very big and famous city called London. London is far away from Africa, in a country called England. The race is called the London Marathon. It's one of the biggest races in the world! Many, many thousands of runners from all over the globe go there to run their best and compete. Imagine thousands of people running together through the streets of a huge city! There are crowds of cheering people all along the way, waving flags and shouting for the runners. It's a very exciting and noisy day! Sabastian was there, ready to run his best. He tied his shoelaces tight. He took a deep breath. And then, when the start gun fired, he ran! He ran past tall buildings and famous landmarks. He ran past cheering crowds, feeling strong and determined. He ran faster and faster, like a gazelle chasing the wind across the African plains! Did you know? Gazelles are super-fast animals that live right here in Africa! They can run incredibly quickly to find food or escape danger. They are natural runners!

Making History for Kenya!

When Sabastian Sawe crossed the finish line, the crowds roared! Everyone cheered loudly! He had done it! He ran faster than two hours! This means Sabastian Sawe didn't just win; he made history! Making history means he did something so special and so new that people will remember it for a very, very, very long time. It's like remembering the day you first learned to ride your bicycle all by yourself! He brought so much joy, excitement, and pride to his home country, Kenya. And not just Kenya, but to all of Africa! He showed everyone how strong, dedicated, and amazing African runners truly are. His incredible run shows all of us that with lots of hard work, practice, and believing in yourself, you can achieve extraordinary things. Even things that many people thought were impossible! Did you know? Running is super good for your body and your mind! It makes your heart strong, helps you sleep better, and can even help you think more clearly in school!

Why This is Super Special

Sabastian's super-fast run is special for many big reasons. First, it shows that people can always keep getting better and faster. It pushes what we thought was possible for humans to do. It makes us dream bigger! Second, it makes all of Africa proud! When one of us does something so amazing on the world stage, we all feel happy and full of pride for our continent. Third, and maybe most importantly, it inspires other kids, maybe even you! It shows that if you dream big, work very hard, and never give up, you can achieve your goals too, no matter how big they seem. Maybe one day, you will be a super runner like Sabastian! Or a super scientist! Or a super artist! Anything is possible!

You Can Run Too!

You don't have to run a whole marathon to be a runner! You can run in your garden. You can run with your friends in the park. You can even run to school with your classmates! Running is fun! It makes you feel strong, healthy, and happy. So next time you're playing outside, remember Sabastian Sawe from Kenya. Remember that little steps and continuous effort can lead to very big and amazing achievements! Always try your best, just like Sabastian. Who knows what wonderful and historic things you will do! Keep moving, keep smiling, and keep shining, Africa!
summary: Sabastian Sawe, a super runner from Kenya, Africa, made history at the London Marathon by becoming the first person to finish the incredibly long race in less than two hours in a big competition, making his country and continent very proud!
whyMatters: This is important because Sabastian's amazing run shows everyone that with hard work and belief, you can do things nobody thought possible, inspiring kids across Africa to dream big and work hard for their goals.
